text: [Music]
today I'm going to make custard pudding
I think I might add this one to my
notebook cookbook I started keeping it
when I was young when I first started
cooking it was the best advice I was
ever given and I always encourage my
maids to do the same usually I make
custard pudding with isinglass but the
grocer in Wilton didn't have any so
today I'm going to have to use gelatine
I hope it works for this recipe you will
need cream
gelatine
[Music]
egg yolks orange flower water brandy
and black currant jam now to make an
excellent custard firstly I've taken a
quarter of the cream heated it a little
and melted the gelatine now I'm going to
whisk some eggs simple sweets like this
are eaten in the second course Lord and
Lady brave Rach are very fond of custard
in fact I think Lord brave work would
eat it with everything now these are
well whipped
I'm going to add the rest of the cold
cream and now the warm cream and now to
make this thicken I need to put it over
a low heat
[Music]
you need to heat it in order to thicken
it but you must be careful not to curdle
it
[Music]
now the custard is thoroughly cool I can
add the brandy and a little orange
flower water is quite an old-fashioned
flavor and scent I'm only going to use a
little because it can be quite
overpowering and I wouldn't want to
cheapen it it's now time to put the
custard into the mold I've already lined
it with almond oil so all I need to do
is to pour the custard in
very good
I'm now going to put it in the icebox
[Music]
now my custard is set it's time to D
mold it I've heated up a little black
currant jam with some port and ginger
wine and put it through a sieve I'm now
going to pour it just around the side of
the custard I must make sure it's not
too hot I don't want it to melt the
custard
[Music]
and there we have it my custard pudding
[Music]
you
